rulings.Case Title: SHANEL STASZ VS. ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Further Responses To Judgemnt Debtor Interrogatories From Pltf And For Monetary Sanctions Gainst Her
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 5, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT MICHAEL EISENBERG'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judgment Debtor Interrogatories From Plaintiff And For Monetary Sanctions against Her. HEARING REQUIRED. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO APPEAR. DEFENDANT MUST BE PREPARED TO SERVE JUDGMENT DEBTOR AT THE HEARING. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 5, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT JUN TAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS ADMITTED THAT HE WAS NOT A LICENSED CONTRACTOR IN RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION #4. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT IS DEFECTIVE AND THERE IS NO BASIS FOR A CONTINUANCE AND NO DECLARATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 437c(h). ALL OF THE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT THE PARTIES ENTERED INTO A CONSTRUCTION CONTRACT AND NOT A JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS VS. POST CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel The Responses Of Deft Post Construction Services,L.P. To Pltf'S Written Discovery And For Related Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 5, 2011, line 4, PLAINTIFF JAMES S. MADOW, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF HIS, Motion To Compel The Responses Of Defendant Post Construction Services, L.P. To Plaintiff's Written Discovery And For Related Relief. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED. THE COURT APPOINTS A DISCOVERY REFEREE SUA SPONTE. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER TO SELECT A DISCOVERY REFEREE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE THEY SHALL EACH NOMINATE TWO REFEREES AND APPEAR EX PARTE FOR THE COURT TO SELECT WHO WILL BE APPOINTED. THE PARTIES SHALL EQUALLY SPLIT THE COST OF THE REFEREE. =(302/LMG)
